name: trade_policy_workplace_injury_spike_oakline_jus_mer
premise: A Trade Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving trade policy workplace injury spike oakline jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Mateo Patel
agents.identity: I am Mateo Patel, accountable for a defensible call in Trade Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Elena Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Elena Vasquez, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Trade Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Mateo Patel must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Mateo Patel do?
